title: Systems and methods for embolization of body structures
abstract: A device for treatment of a vascular defect within a patient's vasculature includes a self-expanding permeable shell having a proximal end, a distal end, and a longitudinal axis, the shell comprising a plurality of elongate resilient filaments having a braided structure, wherein the filaments are secured at at least one of the proximal end or the distal end of the permeable shell, wherein the permeable shell has a radially constrained elongated state configured for delivery within a microcatheter and has an expanded state with an axially shortened configuration relative to the radially constrained state, the permeable shell having a plurality of openings formed between the braided filaments, wherein the permeable shell in its expanded state comprises a plurality of circumferentially-arrayed lobes.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1 . A device for treatment of a vascular defect within a patient's vasculature, comprising: a self-expanding permeable shell having a proximal end, a distal end, and a longitudinal axis, the shell comprising a plurality of elongate resilient filaments having a braided structure, wherein the filaments are secured at at least one of the proximal end or the distal end of the permeable shell, wherein the permeable shell has a radially constrained elongated state configured for delivery within a microcatheter and has an expanded state with an axially shortened configuration relative to the radially constrained state, the permeable shell having a plurality of openings formed between the braided filaments; wherein the permeable shell in its expanded state comprises a plurality of circumferentially-arrayed lobes. 2. The device of claim 1 , wherein the circumferentially-arrayed lobes are evenly distributed around the longitudinal axis of the permeable shell. 3. The device of either one of claims 1 or 2, wherein the permeable shell comprises between two and sixteen circumferentially-arrayed lobes. 4. The device of claim 3, wherein the permeable shell comprises four circumferentially-arrayed lobes. 5. The device of claim 3, wherein the permeable shell comprises six circumferentially-arrayed lobes. 6. The device of claim 3, wherein the permeable shell comprises eight circumferentially-arrayed lobes. 7. The device of any one of claims 1 -6, wherein each of the circumferentially- arrayed lobes extends longitudinally substantially between the proximal end and the distal end of the permeable shell. 8. The device of any one of claims 1 -7, further comprising a plurality of longitudinal channels, each channel extending between two of the circumferentially-arrayed lobes. 9. The device of claim 8, wherein the number of channels is the same as the number of circumferentially-arrayed lobes. 10. The device of either of claims 8 or 9, wherein each channel extends longitudinally substantially between the proximal end and the distal end of the permeable shell. 1 1 . The device of any one of claims 8-10, wherein the permeable shell is configured to pleat along each of the channels. 12. The device of any one of claims 1 -1 1 , wherein the permeable shell in its expanded state has a major diameter D, and wherein each of the plurality of circumferentially-arrayed lobes has a radius r, wherein r < D/2. 13. The device of any one of claims 1 -12, wherein the filaments are secured at the distal end of the permeable shell. 14. The device of any one of claims 1 -12, wherein the filaments are secured at the proximal end of the permeable shell. 15. The device of claim 14, wherein each of the plurality of elongate resilient filaments has a first end, a central section, and a second end, wherein the first and second ends of the plurality of filaments are secured at the proximal end of the permeable shell, and wherein the central section of each of the plurality of filaments passes through a distal region of the permeable shell. 16. The device of claim 15, wherein the plurality of elongate filaments are not secured together at the distal end of the permeable shell. 17. The device of any one of claims 1 -16, wherein the plurality of filaments comprises filaments of at least two different transverse dimensions. 18. The device of any one of claims 1 -17, wherein at least some of the plurality of filaments comprise platinum. 19. The device of any one of claims 1 -18, wherein at least some of the plurality of filaments comprise nitinol. 20. The device of any one of claims 1 -19, wherein at least some of the plurality of filaments are drawn filled tubes. 21 . The device of any one of claims 1 -20, wherein each of the plurality of filaments has a transverse dimension of between about 0.0005 inches and about 0.002 inches. 22. The device of claim 21 , wherein each of the plurality of filaments has a transverse dimension of between about 0.00075 inches and about 0.00125 inches. 23. The device of any one of claims 1 -22, wherein the permeable shell in its expanded state has a major diameter D which is between about two millimeters and about fourteen millimeters. 24. The device of claim 23, wherein the permeable shell in its expanded state has a major diameter D which is between about three millimeters and about twelve millimeters. 25. The device of any one of claims 1 -24, wherein the permeable shell in its expanded state has a length of between about two millimeters and about ten millimeters. 26. The device of any one of claims 1 -1 1 and 13-25, wherein each of the plurality of circumferentially-arrayed lobes comprises a first side having a first radius, a second side having a second radius and a central portion between the first side and the second side. 27. The device of claim 26, wherein the first radius is about the same as the second radius. 28. The device of either of claims 26 or 27, wherein the central portion comprises a third radius. 29. The device of claim 28, wherein the third radius is greater than each of the first radius and the second radius. 30. The device of claim 29, wherein the third radius is about equal to one-half of a major diameter D of the permeable shell. 31 . The device of any one of claims 8-1 1 , wherein the permeable shell has a major diameter D, and wherein the radial compression resistance of the permeable shell is higher than the radial compression resistance of a circular cross-section, non-lobed permeable shell having a diameter equal to D, wherein the circular cross-section, non- lobed permeable shell having diameter D is formed of an identical number a filaments of identical transverse dimensions and materials as the permeable shell. 32. The device of any one of claims 8-1 1 or 31 , wherein the permeable shell has a major diameter D, and wherein the bending stiffness of a wall of one of the plurality of circumferentially-arrayed lobes is higher than the bending stiffness of a wall of a circular cross-section, non-lobed permeable shell having a diameter equal to D, wherein the 

 circular cross-section, non-lobed permeable shell having diameter D is formed of an identical number a filaments of identical transverse dimensions and materials as the permeable shell. 33. The device of either of claims 31 or 32, wherein the bending stiffness of a wall of one of the plurality of longitudinal channels is higher than the bending stiffness of a wall of a circular cross-section, non-lobed permeable shell having a diameter equal to D, wherein the circular cross-section, non-lobed permeable shell having diameter D is formed of an identical number of filaments of identical transverse dimensions and materials as the permeable shell. 34. The device of any one of claims 31 -33, wherein a cross-section of the permeable shell in its expanded state in a plane perpendicular to the longitudinal axis has a first outer perimeter, and wherein a cross-section of the circular cross-section, non-lobed permeable shell having diameter D and formed of an identical number of filaments of identical transverse dimensions and materials as the permeable shell in its expanded state in a plane perpendicular to its longitudinal axis has a second outer perimeter, the second outer perimeter less than the first outer perimeter. 35. The device of any of claims 8-1 1 , wherein the permeable shell has a major diameter D, and wherein the permeable shell has a minor diameter d, defined by the plurality of longitudinal channels, and wherein the ratio between the major diameter and the minor diameter is between about 1 .05 and about 1 .35. 36. The device of claim 35, wherein the ratio between the major diameter and the minor diameter is between about 1 .15 and about 1 .25. 37. The device of claim 36, wherein the ratio between the major diameter and the minor diameter is about 1 .20. 38. The device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the permeable shell further comprises at least one recess extending circumferentially around at least a portion of a perimeter of the permeable shell. 39. The device of claim 38, wherein the at least one recess comprises a continuous indented ring extending 360 degrees around the longitudinal axis. 40. The device of claim 38, wherein the at least one recess comprises a first recess and a second recess. 41 . The device of claim 40, wherein the first recess and the second recess are at different locations along the longitudinal axis. 42. The device of claim 40, wherein the first recess and the second recess each extend less than 180 degrees around the longitudinal axis. 43. The device of any one of claims 38-42, wherein the at least one recess has a substantially semi-circular cross-section. 44. The device of any one of claims 38-42, wherein the at least one recess has a substantially triangular cross-section. 45. A device for treatment of a vascular defect within a patient's vasculature, comprising: a self-expanding permeable shell having a proximal end, a distal end, and a longitudinal axis, the shell comprising a plurality of elongate resilient filaments having a braided structure, wherein the filaments are secured at at least one of the proximal end or the distal end of the permeable shell, wherein the permeable shell has a radially constrained elongated state configured for delivery within a microcatheter and has an expanded state with an axially shortened configuration relative to the radially 

 constrained state, the permeable shell having a plurality of openings formed between the braided filaments; wherein the permeable shell in its expanded state comprises at least one recess extending circumferentially around at least a portion of a perimeter of the permeable shell. 46. The device of claim 45, wherein the at least one recess comprises a continuous indented ring extending 360 degrees around the longitudinal axis. 47. The device of claim 45, wherein the at least one recess comprises a first recess and a second recess. 48. The device of claim 47, wherein the first recess and the second recess are at different locations along the longitudinal axis. 49. The device of claim 47, wherein the first recess and the second recess each extend less than 180 degrees around the longitudinal axis. 50. The device of any one of claims 45-49, wherein the at least one recess has a substantially semi-circular cross-section. 51 . The device of any one of claims 45-49, wherein the at least one recess has a substantially triangular cross-section.
